# Swashbuckler Class Feats — Player Core 2 (PF2e Remastered)

Complete reference of all Swashbuckler class feats from Player Core 2 (PC2) and supplementary sources, levels 1–10.

Source: [Archives of Nethys](https://2e.aonprd.com/) — Player Core 2 pg. 157–167 (version 1.1)

---

## Level 1 (10 feats)

### Disarming Flair
- **ID:** 6126 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 157
- **Description:** You can show off by making flashy disarms. You gain the bravado ability on your Disarm action (Athletics vs. Reflex DC). Your Disarm also gains the bravado trait.

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

### Elegant Buckler
- **ID:** 6127 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 157
- **Description:** You are trained in a singular technique that lets you gain more than the usual benefit from a buckler. When you Raise a Shield while wielding a buckler, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC instead of the buckler's normal shield bonus. If you have panache and an enemy makes an attack roll against you that results in a critical failure, you gain panache.

### Extravagant Parry
- **ID:** 6128 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Requirements:** You are wielding a melee weapon in one hand and your other hand is free
- **Description:** You parry oncoming attacks with your weapon. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn as long as you continue to meet the requirements. If you have panache, the circumstance bonus increases to +2. If an enemy makes an attack roll against you while you have panache that results in a critical failure, you gain panache.

### Flashy Dodge
- **ID:** 6129 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Trigger:** You are targeted by an attack and you can see the attacker
- **Description:** You dodge aside flamboyantly. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses you, you gain panache.
- **Leads To:** Flashy Roll

### Flying Blade
- **ID:** 6130 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Description:** You've learned to apply your flashy techniques to thrown weapons as well as melee attacks. When you have panache and make a thrown weapon Strike, you can apply your precise strike damage. In addition, all of your finisher actions can be performed with a thrown weapon in addition to a melee weapon. Since a thrown weapon is a ranged attack, this doesn't allow you to use Confident Finisher or other finishers on a target that is not within reach.

### Focused Fascination
- **ID:** 6131 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Description:** Even your less impressive social performances captivate the audience. When you Fascinate a target using the appropriate stylish combatant action for your style and get a success (but not a critical success), you fascinate the target, but only until the end of your next turn (instead of the normal duration for a critical success).

### Goading Feint
- **ID:** 6132 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Deception
- **Description:** Your Feints have an extra effect. When you successfully Feint, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against creatures other than you until the beginning of your next turn. On a critical success, the penalty is –3.

### One For All
- **ID:** 6133 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Bravado, Linguistic, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Diplomacy
- **Description:** With a dramatic flourish, you call encouragement to an ally. Attempt a Diplomacy check. The DC is usually 20, though the GM might adjust it based on the circumstances. If the creature you're giving encouragement to is at least 2 levels lower than you, it gets an automatic success on this check. The creature must be able to see and appreciate your performance. If there is a check associated with the Aid reaction that creates a circumstance bonus, it replaces the +1 circumstance bonus from Aid with the result of this action instead.

**Critical Success** The ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the first attack roll or skill check it attempts before the start of your next turn.
**Success** The ally gains a +1 circumstance bonus.
**Critical Failure** The ally takes a –1 circumstance penalty.

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

### Plummeting Roll
- **ID:** 6134 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Description:** You roll with a fall to minimize damage using your panache. You gain the Cat Fall skill feat. In addition, when you fall, you can use a reaction to Stride up to 10 feet after you land (or after you take damage, if you take damage from a fall). You must end this Stride on a surface that can support you, and you don't take damage from the fall for the distance of this additional Stride.

### You're Next
- **ID:** 4922 | **Level:** 1
- **Traits:** Emotion, Fear, Mental, Rogue, Swashbuckler, Visual
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 171, Player Core 2 pg. 165
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Intimidation
- **Trigger:** You reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points
- **Description:** After downing a foe, you menace another. Attempt to Demoralize a creature within 60 feet, with a +2 circumstance bonus. If you have legendary proficiency in Intimidation, you can use this as a free action with the same trigger.

---

## Level 2 (10 feats)

### After You
- **ID:** 6135 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬡ (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Trigger:** You roll initiative
- **Description:** You step aside gracefully, letting others take the lead — but only to make a more dramatic entrance. If you choose to go last in the initiative order for the first round of combat (placing your initiative at 0), you gain panache.

### Antagonize
- **ID:** 6136 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Emotion, Fear, Mental, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Description:** You keep pressure on a frightened foe. When a creature that has the frightened condition due to your actions would reduce its frightened value, it can't reduce below frightened 1 until it takes a hostile action against you. This effect ends automatically at the end of your next turn if the target couldn't see you at any point during that time.

### Brandishing Draw
- **ID:** 6137 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 158
- **Description:** You draw your weapon with a menacing flourish. You Interact to draw a weapon, then make a melee Strike or use a finisher with the weapon you drew.

### Charmed Life
- **ID:** 6138 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Fortune, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Frequency:** once per day
- **Trigger:** You attempt a saving throw
- **Description:** You seem to have a knack for getting out of sticky situations. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the triggering saving throw. If you succeed, you gain panache.

### Enjoy the Show
- **ID:** 6139 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Bravado, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You can humiliate foes with your flashy performance. You gain the bravado ability on your Perform check when used as part of your stylish combatant action for the wit style. After using Perform, designate one enemy within 30 feet that witnessed your performance. That enemy takes a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls against creatures other than you until the end of your next turn. If you have panache, the penalty is –2.

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

### Fane's Fourberie *(Uncommon)*
- **ID:** 2266 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Rogue, Stance, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** PFS Guide pg. 118
- **Access:** Member of the Pathfinder Society
- **Description:** Stella Fane has perfected a technique for using playing cards as weapons that's one part sleight of hand and one part magic. When you enter this stance, choose whether to treat playing cards in your possession as daggers or darts; you can wield a playing card in all ways as the chosen weapon until the stance ends. As long as the majority of the deck remains in your possession, any cards that are lost or thrown can be found after 1 minute. Otherwise, the remaining cards are likely lost or destroyed.

A character who has this feat can enchant a single deck of playing cards as a magic weapon, etching fundamental and property runes directly onto the deck of cards.

### Finishing Follow-through
- **ID:** 6140 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You regain panache quickly after a successful finisher finishes the job. If a finisher you use reduces a foe to 0 Hit Points, you gain panache.

### Retreating Finisher
- **ID:** 6141 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You make a graceful departure from an enemy. Make a melee Strike, then Step. If the Strike hits, you can Step a second time.

### Tumble Behind (Swashbuckler)
- **ID:** 6142 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You tumble under and behind your foe to catch them off guard. When you successfully Tumble Through, the foe whose space you passed through is off-guard against the next attack you make before the end of your turn.

### Unbalancing Finisher
- **ID:** 6143 | **Level:** 2
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You attack a foe and leave them unsteady. Make a melee Strike. If it hits, the target is off-guard until the end of your next turn.

---

## Level 4 (10 feats)

### Dastardly Dash
- **ID:** 6144 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Flourish, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Description:** You dash up to a foe and kick sand in their eyes, trip them, or use some other dirty trick. Stride up to half your Speed. At the end of your Stride, attempt a Trip or Dirty Trick against an adjacent creature. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't apply until after this action.

### Devrin's Dazzling Diversion *(Uncommon)*
- **ID:** 4126 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Rogue, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Firebrands pg. 76
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Deception
- **Access:** Members of the Firebrands at the rank of second mark or higher have access to this feat.
- **Description:** Devrin Arlos has a habit of using whatever is at hand to temporarily blind others in combat. Copying his technique, you make use of your cloak, the light reflecting from your weapon, a handful of flower petals, or some other physical distraction as part of your feint, leaving your opponent unable to see for a moment. When you successfully Feint a creature, it becomes dazzled until the end of your turn. If you critically succeed, the creature is dazzled until the start of your next turn. The creature can use an Interaction to remove the dazzled condition. As normal, you can dazzle only creatures that use vision or a similar sense, as determined by the GM.

### Even the Odds
- **ID:** 6145 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬡ (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Frequency:** once per 10 minutes
- **Trigger:** An enemy flanks you or you become off-guard to an enemy
- **Description:** You immediately gain panache. You can also Interact to draw a weapon as part of this reaction.

### Flamboyant Athlete
- **ID:** 6146 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 159
- **Prerequisites:** Expert in Athletics
- **Description:** Your panache fuels your physical prowess. You gain a climb Speed and a swim Speed equal to half your Speed. The DCs for your High Jump and Long Jump are reduced by 5 when you have panache.

### Guardian's Deflection
- **ID:** 6147 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Trigger:** An ally within your melee reach is hit by an attack, you can see the attacker, and the ally gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC would turn the hit into a miss
- **Description:** You use your weapon or body to deflect an attack that would have hit an ally. The ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If you have panache, you gain panache after using this reaction (consuming your panache-granting opportunity for this turn).

### Impaling Finisher
- **ID:** 6148 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You stab two foes on the same weapon. Make a melee Strike with a piercing weapon and compare the attack roll to the AC of up to two foes, each of whom must be in your melee reach and adjacent to each other. Roll damage only once and apply it to each creature you hit. An Impaling Finisher counts as two attacks for your multiple attack penalty.

### Leading Dance
- **ID:** 6149 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Bravado, Move, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You sweep your foe into a dance. Attempt a Performance check against the target's Will DC.

**Critical Success** The target is moved up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice, and you can move with it (staying adjacent). Then, the target is off-guard until the end of your next turn.
**Success** The target is moved up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice, and you can move with it (staying adjacent).
**Failure** Nothing happens.
**Critical Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

### Masquerade of Seasons Stance *(Rare)*
- **ID:** 4044 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Rare, Bard, Rogue, Stance, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Impossible Lands pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Performance
- **Description:** You have mastered the opening steps to the Masquerade of Seasons, a dance that channels the power of spring, summer, winter, and autumn. Choose one of the following traits: water, fire, negative, or cold. You gain resistance 5 to damage dealt by effects with the chosen trait. You can use an action to reenter this stance at any time, changing the granted resistance to another of the available options. If you are at least 12th level, the granted resistance increases to be equal to half your level.
- **Leads To:** Pirouette, Grand Dance

### Swaggering Initiative
- **ID:** 6150 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬡ (free action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Trigger:** You roll initiative
- **Description:** You stride confidently into battle, immediately commanding attention. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to initiative, and you can Interact to draw a weapon. If you roll a success on your initiative check, you gain panache regardless of whether you go first.

### Twirling Throw
- **ID:** 6151 | **Level:** 4
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You hurl your weapon with a dramatic spin. Make a thrown Strike with a melee weapon. You don't take the –2 circumstance penalty for making a ranged Strike with a thrown weapon that doesn't have the thrown trait, and you ignore the range penalty for the first range increment. Your weapon returns to you after the Strike, even if it doesn't have the returning rune. You must have a free hand to catch it.

---

## Level 6 (7 feats)

### Agile Maneuvers
- **ID:** 6152 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 160
- **Description:** You easily maneuver against your foes. Your Disarm, Grapple, Reposition, Shove, and Trip actions have a lower multiple attack penalty. Even if your weapon or unarmed attack isn't agile, the multiple attack penalty for these actions is –4 on the second attack on your turn and –8 if it's your third or subsequent attack. If your weapon or unarmed attack is agile and you have panache, the penalty is reduced further, to –3 if it's the second attack on your turn or –6 if it's the third or subsequent.

### Combination Finisher
- **ID:** 6153 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You've practiced the art of flowing from one attack into a finisher. When you make a melee Strike as part of a finisher action, if your previous action this turn was also a melee Strike, the finisher Strike takes a –3 multiple attack penalty instead of a –5 multiple attack penalty (or a –2 instead of –4 with an agile weapon).

### Pirouette *(Rare)*
- **ID:** 4045 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Rare, Bard, Rogue, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Impossible Lands pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Masquerade of Seasons Stance
- **Trigger:** An enemy targets you with a Strike
- **Requirements:** You're in Masquerade of Seasons Stance
- **Description:** You twirl gracefully, spinning on one foot to evade your opponent's attack. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses you, you can immediately Step. If the attack hits you anyway, reduce the damage it deals by an amount equal to half your level; this reduction in damage is applied after calculating immunities, resistances, and weakness.

### Precise Finisher
- **ID:** 6154 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You've trained to make your finishers more accurate. When you use Confident Finisher and the Strike fails (but doesn't critically fail), you still deal your full precise strike damage, rather than only half.

### Reactive Strike
- **ID:** 5832 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Barbarian, Champion, Commander, Exemplar, Guardian, Magus, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161 (and other class sources)
- **Trigger:** A creature within your reach uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using
- **Description:** You lash out at a foe that leaves an opening. Make a melee Strike against the triggering creature. If your attack is a critical hit and the trigger was a manipulate action, you disrupt that action. This Strike doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, and your multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this Strike.

### Stella's Stab and Snag *(Uncommon)*
- **ID:** 2268 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Rogue, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** PFS Guide pg. 118
- **Prerequisites:** Expert in Thievery
- **Access:** Member of the Pathfinder Society
- **Requirements:** You are wielding a piercing or slashing melee weapon
- **Description:** You dart towards your opponent, seeking to stab them and slice their purse-strings with a single movement. Stride up to your Speed; this movement does not trigger reactions triggered by movement. At any point during this movement you can Strike an opponent within reach. If this Strike is successful, you can attempt a Thievery check to Steal from the target.

### Vexing Tumble
- **ID:** 6155 | **Level:** 6
- **Traits:** Bravado, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You tumble past a foe in a way that confuses and distracts them. Stride up to half your Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions. At the end of your Stride, attempt an Acrobatics check against the Reflex DC of an adjacent foe. On a success, the foe can't use reactions until the end of your next turn (on a critical success, it also becomes off-guard until the start of your next turn).

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

---

## Level 8 (9 feats)

### Bleeding Finisher
- **ID:** 6156 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You inflict a wound that leaves your foe bleeding. Make a melee Strike. If you hit, the target takes persistent bleed damage equal to the number of damage dice from your precise strike. (For example, if your precise strike damage is 3d6, the target takes 3 persistent bleed damage.)

### Distracting Toss
- **ID:** 6157 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Bravado, Flourish, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Description:** You hurl a weapon in a distracting arc. You Feint against a creature within 30 feet, using your thrown weapon attack modifier instead of Deception. This Feint gains the range trait. On any result other than a critical failure, you then make a thrown Strike against the target. The feint in this case doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the Strike does.

**Bravado — Critical Success** You gain panache.
**Bravado — Failure** You are off-guard until the start of your next turn.

### Dual Finisher
- **ID:** 6158 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Finisher, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Requirements:** You are wielding two melee weapons, each in a different hand
- **Description:** You make two melee Strikes, one with each required weapon, each against a different foe. Your multiple attack penalty applies normally for these Strikes. Both Strikes can apply the damage from your precise strike, but the second strike applies only half the number of weapon damage dice from your precise strike.

### Flamboyant Cruelty *(Rare)*
- **ID:** 2161 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Rare, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Legends pg. 28
- **Description:** You love to kick your enemies when they're down, and you look fabulous when you do. When you make a melee weapon Strike against a foe that has at least two of the following conditions, you gain a circumstance bonus to your damage roll equal to the number of these conditions the foe has. The qualifying conditions are clumsy, drained, enfeebled, frightened, sickened, and stupefied. If you hit such a foe, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks to Tumble Through and to perform your style's panache-granting actions until the end of your turn.

### Flashy Roll
- **ID:** 6159 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 161
- **Prerequisites:** Flashy Dodge
- **Description:** You can use Flashy Dodge before attempting a Reflex saving throw in addition to its normal trigger. If you succeed at the save, you gain panache (instead of the normal trigger requirement of the attack missing you). When you use Flashy Dodge against either trigger, you can also Stride up to 10 feet as part of the reaction. You must end this Stride in a space that is not adjacent to the triggering creature (or the creature whose effect caused the Reflex save).

### Grand Dance *(Rare)*
- **ID:** 4046 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Rare, Bard, Rogue, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬺ (2 actions)
- **Source:** Impossible Lands pg. 126
- **Prerequisites:** Masquerade of Seasons Stance
- **Requirements:** You're in Masquerade of Seasons Stance
- **Description:** You can summon the full magic of Bhopan's grand dance. Stride up to your Speed; during this Stride, you can walk on air as if it were solid ground. You can ascend and descend in this way at a maximum of a 45-degree angle. You must end your Stride on a surface that can support you or you fall.

### Knight's Retaliation *(Uncommon)*
- **ID:** 3635 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Fighter, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Knights of Lastwall pg. 84
- **Prerequisites:** Trained in Athletics
- **Access:** Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.
- **Trigger:** An undead creature within your reach critically fails a Strike against you
- **Description:** You knock the enemy's blow aside and twist, unbalancing your foe. You attempt to Disarm, Shove, or Trip the triggering creature.

### Stunning Finisher
- **ID:** 6160 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Finisher, Incapacitation, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 162
- **Description:** You put the finishing touches on your foe with a stunning blow. Make a melee Strike. If you hit, the foe must attempt a Fortitude save against your class DC.

**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is stunned 1.
**Failure** The target is stunned 3.
**Critical Failure** The target is stunned 4.

### Vivacious Bravado
- **ID:** 6161 | **Level:** 8
- **Traits:** Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 162
- **Requirements:** You gained panache during this turn (before using this action)
- **Description:** You channel the thrill of combat into staying power. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level plus your Charisma modifier. These temporary Hit Points last until the start of your next turn.

---

## Level 10 (4 feats)

### Buckler Dance
- **ID:** 6162 | **Level:** 10
- **Traits:** Stance, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 162
- **Requirements:** You are wielding a buckler
- **Description:** You constantly have your buckler raised as if you'd used Raise a Shield. You can use the Shield Block reaction with your buckler as long as you're in this stance, even if you don't have that feat.

### Corpse-Killer's Defiance *(Uncommon)*
- **ID:** 3638 | **Level:** 10
- **Traits:** Uncommon, Barbarian, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬲ (reaction)
- **Source:** Knights of Lastwall pg. 85
- **Access:** Knights of Lastwall have access to this feat.
- **Trigger:** You destroy an undead foe
- **Description:** You defy Tar-Baphon with every undead you destroy, using your victory to rally your allies. Allies that can see you gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against undead for 1 round, or a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls against undead for 2 rounds if the undead foe destroyed was a creature of your level or higher.

### Dazzling Display
- **ID:** 4131 | **Level:** 10
- **Traits:** Fighter, Ranger, Rogue, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** ⬻ (1 action)
- **Source:** Firebrands pg. 77
- **Prerequisites:** Expert in Intimidation
- **Description:** You perform a bewildering show of prowess, such as by whirling and flashing a weapon, that unnerves foes. Attempt Intimidation checks to Demoralize each enemy within 30 feet. If your last action was a critical hit against an enemy or reduced an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Intimidation checks. Regardless of the results of your checks, each creature is then temporarily immune to Dazzling Display for 1 minute.

### Derring-do
- **ID:** 6163 | **Level:** 10
- **Traits:** Fortune, Swashbuckler
- **Action:** —
- **Source:** Player Core 2 pg. 162
- **Description:** When you have panache, you roll twice and use the higher result on checks to which the circumstance bonus from your stylish combatant class feature applies (such as Tumble Through for all swashbucklers, and the relevant skill actions for your specific style). This also applies to any skill action that gains the bravado trait from one of your feats.

---

## Summary

| Level | Count |
|-------|-------|
| 1 | 10 |
| 2 | 10 |
| 4 | 10 |
| 6 | 7 |
| 8 | 9 |
| 10 | 4 |
| **Total** | **50** |

---

## Remaster-Specific Changes (Player Core 2 vs. Advanced Player's Guide)

The Swashbuckler class was fully remastered in Player Core 2. Key changes to feats include:

### Renamed Feats
| Legacy (APG) Name | Remaster (PC2) Name | Level |
|--------------------|---------------------|-------|
| Nimble Dodge | Flashy Dodge | 1 |
| Dueling Parry (Swashbuckler) | Extravagant Parry | 1 |
| Buckler Expertise | Elegant Buckler | 1 |
| Attack of Opportunity | Reactive Strike | 6 |
| Nimble Roll | Flashy Roll | 8 |

### New Feats (added in PC2, no APG equivalent)
- **Level 1:** Disarming Flair, Plummeting Roll
- **Level 2:** Brandishing Draw, Enjoy the Show, Finishing Follow-through, Retreating Finisher
- **Level 4:** Dastardly Dash, Even the Odds, Swaggering Initiative, Twirling Throw
- **Level 6:** Agile Maneuvers, Combination Finisher, Vexing Tumble
- **Level 8:** Distracting Toss, Flashy Roll (expanded from Nimble Roll), Stunning Finisher, Vivacious Bravado
- **Level 10:** Buckler Dance

### Removed/Relocated Feats (in APG but not in PC2 L1–10)
- **Gymnastic Poise** (L8) — removed
- **Panache Paragon** (L4) — removed
- **Lethal Finisher** (L8) — removed; similar function folded into other finishers

### Notable Mechanical Changes
- **Bravado trait** (new): Several feats now use the bravado system — on critical success you gain panache, on failure you become off-guard until the start of your next turn.
- **Finisher trait** refined: Finisher actions now more consistently interact with precise strike damage, with clearer rules on when full vs. half damage applies.
- **Precise Finisher** (L6): Now explicitly grants full precise strike damage on a non-critical failure of Confident Finisher (previously ambiguous).
- **You're Next**: Now shared between Rogue and Swashbuckler class feat lists (appeared in both Player Core and Player Core 2).
- **Reactive Strike**: Now explicitly a Swashbuckler class feat option (previously only available via multiclass archetypes or Fighter dedication).
